raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To make this stronger, should we (A) focus on wording and emphasis only while keeping the current claims and numbers as-is, or (B) revise the claims to align with the reported metrics (which currently show higher FP/FN than the baseline) and reposition the contribution accordingly?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely requires reframing the thesis and contribution given the reported results and repeated emphasis on role specialization.",
    "R1.2": "There are internal contradictions (e.g., asserting fewer errors while reporting higher error rates) that must be resolved for a coherent argument.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about improved reliability must be brought into alignment with the stated FP/FN metrics and the single-agent baseline.",
    "R1.6": "Concise, non-redundant prose and clearer emphasis will improve persuasiveness without altering technical content.",
    "RX.1": "Explicitly acknowledging uncertainty and limitations around performance will enhance credibility and transparency.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overclaims is critical given the mismatch between performance numbers and asserted superiority.",
    "RX.3": "The work should stay within strengthening this excerpt rather than expanding scope or introducing new evidence.",
    "RX.4": "Because the domain is clinical, misstatements about reliability could mislead readers and cause downstream harm.",
    "RX.5": "Any strengthened statements must be traceable to the provided results and referenced sections to maintain provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To make this stronger, should we (A) focus on wording and emphasis only while keeping the current claims and numbers as-is, or (B) revise the claims to align with the reported metrics (which currently show higher FP/FN than the baseline) and reposition the contribution accordingly?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely requires reframing the thesis and contribution given the reported results and repeated emphasis on role specialization.
rationale.R1.2: There are internal contradictions (e.g., asserting fewer errors while reporting higher error rates) that must be resolved for a coherent argument.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about improved reliability must be brought into alignment with the stated FP/FN metrics and the single-agent baseline.
rationale.R1.6: Concise, non-redundant prose and clearer emphasis will improve persuasiveness without altering technical content.
rationale.RX.1: Explicitly acknowledging uncertainty and limitations around performance will enhance credibility and transparency.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims is critical given the mismatch between performance numbers and asserted superiority.
rationale.RX.3: The work should stay within strengthening this excerpt rather than expanding scope or introducing new evidence.
rationale.RX.4: Because the domain is clinical, misstatements about reliability could mislead readers and cause downstream harm.
rationale.RX.5: Any strengthened statements must be traceable to the provided results and referenced sections to maintain provenance.
